description: The tail: the body's continuation past itself — the dog's flag, the fish's engine, the snake's ending-that-never-quite-ends. Anatomy for every creature that has one.
sound_symbolism: 'tu' marks the body's end and 'li'–'ra' trails away from it, tapering open — the word thins as a tail does.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'lohau tulira rato.' (The dog turns its tail — wags.) 'shalu roe tulira wishe.' (The fish swims with its tail.)

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: The herder read the flock by tails — health, mood, and weather-sense all signaled at the animal's far end.
